summary:
The incidence of metastatic melanoma stays constant because it reflects new cases over time, unaffected by effective treatments. However, prolonged survival from chemotherapies combined with immunotherapies leads to an increasing prevalence—more people live with the disease simultaneously—as shown through established medical literature.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$
